title: SaaS: Workflow Handoffs + Context Switching
friction_templates: Work gets blocked when context is split across channels (Slack, tickets, docs) without a single source of truth.
Handoffs fail when requests move from chat to tickets without clear acceptance criteria or owner acknowledgment.
Priority shifts create silent rework when decision rights are unclear for what gets pulled in next.
Status churn increases when stakeholders request updates outside the workflow system.
Interrupt-driven work prevents deep progress and makes cycle time unpredictable.
decision_rights_templates: Define who decides priority when multiple stakeholders request changes at once.
Define who can mark work 'blocked' and what information is required to unblock it.
Define what qualifies as 'done' before a handoff is accepted by the next role.
Define the escalation path when a ticket sits idle beyond a threshold.
stop_doing_templates: Stop using ad-hoc pings as the primary tracking method.
Stop letting work move forward without acceptance criteria.
Stop maintaining duplicate trackers unless they have a single owner and clear purpose.
experiment_templates: Pick one workflow and enforce a single source of truth for status for 7 days.
Add an 'acceptance checklist' to the handoff step and measure rework reduction.
Set a rule: no status requests outside the workflow tool; instead, update the ticket once daily.
